title: ophrys forestieri subsp. laureotica
intro: Ophrys forestieri subsp. laureotica is a subspecies of orchid, scientifically known as the bee orchid, that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ophrys forestieri subsp. laureotica are said to include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and aiding in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds and fever. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
